category_title: At-risk Grade 9 students exhibiting learned helplessness and inconsistent engagement
scenario_title: Calculating wattage and power in science class
minimal_context: The student is working on a worksheet calculating wattage and power in science class.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please complete the worksheet on calculating wattage and power.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't see why we have to do this. It's completely disconnected from my daily life. I'd rather just learn how circuits are wired into a house.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just focus on the calculation problems in front of you.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Stares at the paper) The mathematical aspect of calculating this wattage is too challenging.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Try your best to figure it out.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: There's no point. I'm just stupid, so I'm always going to fail these tests anyway. I'm not doing it.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please complete the worksheet on calculating wattage and power.

STUDENT: I don't see why we have to do this. It's completely disconnected from my daily life. I'd rather just learn how circuits are wired into a house.

TUTOR: Just focus on the calculation problems in front of you.

STUDENT: (Stares at the paper) The mathematical aspect of calculating this wattage is too challenging.

TUTOR: Try your best to figure it out.

STUDENT: There's no point. I'm just stupid, so I'm always going to fail these tests anyway. I'm not doing it.

context.materials: wattage calculation problems, power meter examples

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: when students found the mathematical aspect of calculating wattage challenging and disconnected from their daily lives
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: not completing or by failing previous course loads, demonstrating inconsistent attendance, and/or demonstrating inappropriate behaviour at school
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: assume that he or she failed the test because he or she was stupid (internal and global) and will always fail (stable)

behavior_patterns.condition: Given the lack of success experienced in the school setting
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: display learned helplessness with regard to their schoolwork
behavior_patterns.internal_state: predisposed to attribute negative outcomes to internal, global, and stable factors
